title: 365 Days to Get Married
altTitles: Kekkon Suru tte, Hontou desu ka
description: Takuya and Rika are quiet and introverted coworkers working at a travel agency who both enjoy living alone and avoiding attention. Their peaceful daily lives suddenly change when the company announces the opening of a new overseas branch and decides to transfer unmarried employees abroad. Neither of them wants to leave Japan or deal with the stress of relocation, so the two come up with an unusual solution — pretending to be engaged and preparing for a fake marriage in order to avoid being selected for the transfer. At first, their relationship is nothing more than an act created to fool their coworkers and managers. However, as they spend more time together pretending to be a real couple, they slowly begin to understand each other on a deeper level. From awkward conversations and unexpected misunderstandings to heartfelt moments of support and care, the line between fake and real emotions gradually starts to blur. While trying to maintain the appearance of a perfect relationship, Takuya and Rika begin questioning whether their fake engagement could eventually turn into genuine love.
